name: NXPD (POLICE)
mission: To enforce the law, maintain order, and protect the citizens of Nexus City through unwavering dedication and tactical superiority.
vision: A self-sustaining judicial ecosystem where crime is met with calculated, overwhelming force, and justice is swiftly delivered.

rules.title: Application – Terms & Conditions
points: I accept all disciplinary actions regarding promotions, suspensions, or dismissals as IC roleplay and will not take them personally.
Upon resignation/dismissal, I will only participate in legal/whitelisted roles. Illegal activity results in strict disciplinary action.
I confirm I have read and fully understood all NXPD Rules and am 18+ years of age.
I possess valid licenses (Bike, Car, Truck, Weapons) and consent to background verification by PD officials.
All provided information is accurate; false details result in permanent disqualification.
I agree to avoid character breaking, gang bias, vengeance behavior, and power gaming.
I will strictly follow all orders from higher authorities and accept my assigned rank without demand.
Voice changers causing discomfort are prohibited. Voice verification is mandatory during interviews.
Selected candidates begin as a Trainee for 3 days; promotions depend on performance evaluation.
Misconduct or disobedience as a trainee results in immediate termination.
No favoritism, friendly RP bias, or 'Bad Cop' behavior is tolerated.
Selling, giving away, or misusing police-issued or confiscated items results in a permanent ban.
Discussing past gang/failed RP scenarios or making defamatory statements is prohibited.
Report rule violations through official OOC channels to High Command after the scene.
Maintain professionalism and respect toward civilians at all times, regardless of provocation.

rules.title: Selection Process Protocol
points: Applications open once monthly for one week only to maintain managed trainee batches.
Preparations include a detailed application checked by the Head of Dept for the 'PD Interview' role.
Interviews are announced via the NXPD Notice Board. Required licenses must be obtained before the session.
Interviews are conducted by two officers from 'Head of Dept' and 'High Command'.
Successful candidates are posted at the end of the application window.
Initial Stage: Trainee. Fresnel/Experienced must buy their own car, weapon, and armory.
Post-initial 3-day training sessions are mandatory for qualification and first promotion.

rules.title: Protocol-01: Traffic Stop Procedure
points: Activate sirens and instruct the driver to pull over safely to a designated area.
Inform dispatch via radio and request backup (10-50).
Approach cautiously: Instruct driver to turn off engine and exit the vehicle safely.
Record vehicle registration and update in system (/100).
Inform driver of the stop reason and verify ID, License, and RC Book.
Restrictions: Search, arrest, or detention for minor violations is prohibited.
Failing to stop: Request backup (10-50). Up to 5 units may engage in pursuit.
Upon apprehension after evasion, search and full investigation are permitted.

rules.title: Protocol-02: Terry Stop Procedure
points: Conduct when reasonable suspicion exists (Warrant or BOLO alert).
Activate sirens and request (10-50) backup immediately.
Maintain conversation with the suspect until backup arrives on scene.
Once backup is present: Order suspect to exit and raise hands.
Detain, handcuff, and clearly state the reason for detention.
Conduct search of suspect and vehicle (trunk/glove box) upon lawful arrest.
Non-compliance: Maximum 5 officers may pursue an evading suspect.

rules.title: Protocol-03: Hostage Handling
points: Initial: Assign a Hostage Handler and established a perimeter with spike strips.
Communication: Maintain direct, in-person negotiation only. Officers must take cover.
Negotiation: Gather intelligence (Suspects, Weapons, Vehicle ID) and identify demands.
Release: Secure safe hostage release; allow suspects to leave only if instructed by Lead Officer.
Post-Rescue: Temporarily cuff hostage for safety, conduct search, and interview.
Final: Transport hostage to safety and rejoin active pursuit or return to station.

rules.title: Key Law Enforcement Terms
points: Suspect: Individual believed to be involved in a crime, but not yet proven guilty.
Criminal: Person proven guilty through evidence, legal proceedings, or confession.
Warrant: Legal document for arrest or search issued when sufficient evidence exists.
BOLO: 'Be On the Lookout' alert for wanted persons, vehicles, or objects of interest.

roles.name: DGP - Director General of Police
roles.desc: Principal Overseer of the Nexus Police Department. Final authority on all policy and command.

roles.name: SDGP - Special Director General of Police
roles.desc: High-level Operations Command and strategic oversight.

roles.name: ADGP - Additional Director General of Police
roles.desc: Support to the DG and managing administrative/tactical support units.

roles.name: IG - Inspector General of Police
roles.desc: Regional supervisor and disciplinary authority for department-wide standards.

roles.name: DIG - Deputy Inspector General of Police
roles.desc: Assistant to IG, managing multiple district operations units.

roles.name: AIG - Assistant Inspector General of Police
roles.desc: Centralized management of specialized tactical/investigation units.

roles.name: SP - Superintendent of Police
roles.desc: District command lead. Responsible for localized force deployment.

roles.name: DSP - Deputy Superintendent of Police
roles.desc: Lead field officer for large-scale operations and special tactical scenes.

roles.name: ASP - Assistant Superintendent of Police
roles.desc: Entry-tier commander overseeing district units and specialized training.

roles.name: CP - Commissioner of Police
roles.desc: High-level supervisory role managing officer conduct and field investigations.

roles.name: DCP - Deputy Commissioner of Police
roles.desc: Secondary supervisory authority overseeing localized task forces.

roles.name: ACP - Assistant Commissioner of Police
roles.desc: Managing frontline patrol squads and ensuring rule compliance.

roles.name: INS – Inspector
roles.desc: Field supervisor and scene lead for complex investigations and pursuits.

roles.name: SI - Sub-Inspector
roles.desc: First-tier of field leadership. Lead officer for routine patrol groups.

roles.name: ASI - Assistant Sub-Inspector
roles.desc: Senior patrol officer and mentor for the enlisted division.

roles.name: HC - Head Constable
roles.desc: Veteran patrolman and field lead for frontline response units.

roles.name: CONS - Constable
roles.desc: Frontline patrol responder. Fully certified law enforcement officer.

roles.name: TR - Trainee
roles.desc: Cadet undergoing 3-day observation and field evaluation Phase.
